subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d and f Block Elements

definitions.definition: The elements which are present in the 4th period and 5th period to 7th period of the periodic table and exhibit a partially filled d subshell are called d block elements.
The elements which are present in the 6th period and 7th period of the periodic table and exhibit a partially filled f subshell are called f block elements.
The elements which are present in the d block of the periodic table and exhibit a partially filled d subshell are called transition elements.
A series of 14 elements with atomic numbers ranging from 57 to 71 are called lanthanides.
A series of 14 elements with atomic numbers ranging from 90 to 103 are called actinides.
The elements which are present in the f block of the periodic table and exhibit a partially filled f subshell are called inner transition elements.

definitions.term: General Characteristics of d Block Elements
definitions.definition: The general characteristics of d block elements are that they are hard, silver white, less electropositive than s block elements and exhibit variable oxidation states.

definitions.term: General Characteristics of f Block Elements
definitions.definition: The general characteristics of f block elements are that they are silvery white, have high melting and boiling points, are less electropositive than s and d block elements and exhibit variable oxidation states.
The contraction in the size of atoms on moving down a group in the periodic table is called inner contraction.
The contraction in the size of lanthanoids on moving down the group is called lanthanoid contraction.

definitions.term: Magnetic Properties of d Block Elements
definitions.definition: The d block elements are paramagnetic, ferromagnetic or antiferromagnetic in nature.

definitions.term: Magnetic Properties of f Block Elements
definitions.definition: The f block elements are paramagnetic, ferromagnetic or antiferromagnetic in nature.

definitions.term: Oxidation States of d Block Elements
definitions.definition: The oxidation states of d block elements vary from +1 to +7.

definitions.term: Oxidation States of f Block Elements
definitions.definition: The oxidation states of f block elements vary from +3 to +6.
The crystal field theory explains the magnetic and spectroscopic properties of transition metal complexes.

definitions.term: Electronic Spectra of d Block Elements
definitions.definition: The electronic spectra of d block elements provide information about the energy differences between the d orbitals.

definitions.term: Electronic Spectra of f Block Elements
definitions.definition: The electronic spectra of f block elements provide information about the energy differences between the f orbitals.
